entry.title: How to set up and configure Financial Closing Cockpit in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have been tasked with implementing the Financial Closing Cockpit (FCC) in our SAP S/4HANA environment.
I am looking for guidance on the end-to-end setup.
Specifically, I would like to understand:

1. What are the prerequisites and required Customizing steps in SPRO before activating the Financial Closing Cockpit?
2. How is the task list structure set up (task types, dependencies, responsible users/roles)?
3. What is the recommended approach for migrating existing period-end closing activities into the FCC?
4. Are there specific Fiori apps that go along with the FCC, and how are they activated?
5. Any pitfalls or lessons learned from real implementations?

System context: SAP S/4HANA (on-premise), EHP independent.

Any documentation links, blog posts, or step-by-step advice would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Unit of Measurement: EUR / Euro (CUNI)
entry.content:
Hi @AlexSP,
My friend you should not create “EURO” (EUR) as a Unit of Measurement in CUNI. In SAP, currency is never maintained as a unit of measure, and EUR already exists in the system as a currency key, not a UoM. Using EUR as a UoM would break SAP design principles and cause issues in CO, FI, and reporting.

SAP separates Units of Measure (UoM) and Currencies. UoM (CUNI) is for quantities (kg, m, pcs, hours). Currencies (OB08 / TCUR)* are for monetary values (EUR, USD, SAR).
SAP does not allow or recommend mixing these domains. Creating EUR as a UoM would violate this architecture and confuse downstream processes.

entry.title: Which Fiori apps are essential for FI and CO in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,
We are currently transitioning our SAP S/4HANA users from classic SAP GUI transactions to the Fiori Launchpad.
I am responsible for defining which Fiori apps should replace our existing transaction set.

I have the following questions:
1. What are the most important and commonly used Fiori apps for Financial Accounting (FI) — e.g. for document posting, clearing, open item management, and reporting?
2. What are the key Fiori apps for Controlling (CO) — e.g. cost center reporting, internal orders, profitability analysis?
3. Is there an official SAP mapping list of classic transactions (e.g. FB60, FB70, F110, KSB1) to their Fiori app equivalents?
4. What is the recommended approach for a phased Fiori rollout — all at once or area by area?
5. Are there any apps that have known limitations compared to the classic transaction counterpart? We are on SAP S/4HANA on-premise. Any tips, app lists, or migration experiences are very welcome! Thank you!

entry.title: How to configure Intrastat reporting for Germany in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I need to set up Intrastat reporting for Germany (DE) in our SAP S/4HANA system.
We perform intra-EU goods movements and are required to submit monthly Intrastat declarations to the Statistisches Bundesamt (Destatis).
I am looking for guidance on:
1. What are the required Customizing steps in SPRO for Intrastat in S/4HANA (Foreign Trade settings, commodity codes, statistical values, etc.)?
2. How is Intrastat data captured automatically from SD deliveries and MM goods receipts — what needs to be maintained on material and partner master data?
3. Which transaction or Fiori app is used to create and review the Intrastat declaration (previously VE01 / MEIS in ECC)?
4. How is the electronic INSTAT/XML file generated for submission to Destatis?
5. Are there any S/4HANA-specific changes compared to classic ECC Intrastat configuration?
6. How is the statistical value threshold handled and how are corrections submitted?

Any configuration guides, OSS notes, or practical experience from German implementations would be very helpful. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Datasphere: Accessing target structure in Python Script
entry.content:
Hello @mitko1994 ,

The short answer to your question is: No.
The Python editor is incredible simple, and is executed in it's own environment, separate from the data flow in general.  

Another fun note: the dataflow matches your columns on the order in the dataframe, not by texts or keys. 

So, not only can't you access the target structure - you have to manually create it, and make sure the output of your script matches the output exactly. 

Sorry I can't be more helpful 😞

entry.title: The Ultimate Guide: Adding Custom Sort & Filter Buttons to SAP Custom Maintenance Table (SM30)
entry.content:
Have you ever received a "quick" requirement to add a Sort or Filter button to a standard SAP Table Maintenance Generator (TMG)? What sounds like a 10-minute task often spirals into a frustrating battle against disappearing buttons, Unicode short dumps, and custom filters that vanish the moment a user toggles between Display and Change modes.

After wrestling with these exact issues, I developed a bulletproof, universal methodology to add Ascending, Descending, Filter, and Clear buttons to any SM30 screen, while also baking in strict manual data validation.

In this comprehensive guide, I will walk you through the entire process, from configuring the Menu Painter to writing Unicode-safe memory-casting logic.

Part 1: Conquering the GUI Status & TMG Events

Standard TMGs borrow their toolbars from the master program SAPLSVIM. To add our own buttons, we must bring those toolbars into our custom Function Group.

Step 1: Copying the Standard GUI Interface (SE41)

 

  1. Go to transaction SE41.

  2. Enter Program: SAPLSVIM .
    Enter standard program and copy User Interface
  3. Copy the interface to your TMG's main program. Apply the 'SAPL' in front of your TMG's main program. 
    Copy standard interface to custom program

Step 2: Adding the Custom Buttons

Enter customer program (e.g., SAPLZ...) and 'EULG' (Standard Change Mode status) as the status and then click the change button, expand the Application Toolbar, and add your custom function codes: ZASC (Ascending), ZDESC (Descending), ZFIL (Filter), and ZCLR (Clear). Double-click them to assign standard icons (like ICON_SORT_UP).
EULG status

 

Add custom buttons to EULG status

Step 3: The "Red Text" Display Mode Secret

When you open the ESLG (Display Mode) status to add your buttons, the text will turn RED. Do not rename the buttons to bypass this! Red text simply means the button is Inactive. SAP hides editing buttons in Display Mode, and it defaults yours to hidden.

To fix this:

  1. Click the red ZASC text so your cursor is on it.
  2. Then click 'Function Code'.
    Click 'Function Code' to activate existing custom buttons

     

  3.  The text will instantly turn black. Repeat for all four buttons.
    Activated Existing Custom Buttons

     

Step 4: Linking the Toolbar (Event ST)

Now, we must tell the TMG to use our new toolbars.

  1. Go to your TMG (SE11 -> Table -> Utilities -> Table Maintenance Generator).
  2. Navigate to Environment -> Modification -> Events.
    Environment -> Modification -> Events.
  3. Add Event ST (GUI Menu Main Program Name).
  4. Under "Form Routine", enter your Main Program Name (e.g., SAPLZ...).
    Note: Do not enter a FORM routine name; Event ST specifically expects the program name!
    Add event

     

Part 2: The Core Code & Explanations

TMG background tables (extract and total) are untyped, flat character strings. Directly assigning them to structured work areas containing numeric fields causes Unicode dumps. Furthermore, when a user toggles Display <-> Change mode, SAP completely rebuilds the extract table, destroying any active filters.

Here is the complete code to solve both of these massive hurdles.
(Note: Replace ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T with your actual table name).

1. The Screen Flow Logic (SE51)

Developer Note: Timing is everything in screen flow logic. The placement of your modules ensures they don't get overwritten by standard SAP TMG logic.

 

PROCESS BEFORE OUTPUT.
 " Standard SAP initialization
 MODULE LISTE_INITIALISIEREN.
 
 " [CRITICAL] Must be placed immediately AFTER liste_initialisieren. 
 " Standard SAP resets the display mode here. We jump in right after 
 " to re-apply our custom Sort/Filter before the screen renders.
 MODULE REAPPLY_CUSTOM_STATE. 
 
 LOOP AT EXTRACT WITH CONTROL
  TCTRL_ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T CURSOR NEXTLINE.
   MODULE LISTE_SHOW_LISTE.
 ENDLOOP.

PROCESS AFTER INPUT.
 " Standard SAP exit handling (Back, Exit, Cancel)
 MODULE LISTE_EXIT_COMMAND AT EXIT-COMMAND.
 
 " [CRITICAL] Catch our custom button clicks BEFORE the standard loop begins.
 " If we don't catch them here, standard SAP will throw a 'Function Code not found' error.
 MODULE HANDLE_CUSTOM_CMDS.   
 
 MODULE LISTE_BEFORE_LOOP.

 LOOP AT EXTRACT.
   MODULE LISTE_INIT_WORKAREA.
   CHAIN.
    FIELD ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T-VKORG .
    FIELD ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T-VTWEG .
    FIELD ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T-SPART .
    FIELD ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T-KUNNR .
    FIELD ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T-ZAPPROVER .
    MODULE SET_UPDATE_FLAG ON CHAIN-REQUEST.
   ENDCHAIN.
   FIELD VIM_MARKED MODULE LISTE_MARK_CHECKBOX.
   CHAIN.
    FIELD ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T-VKORG .
    FIELD ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T-VTWEG .
    FIELD ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T-SPART .
    FIELD ZSD_SO_APPR_MX_T-KUNNR .
    MODULE LISTE_UPDATE_LISTE.
   ENDCHAIN.
 ENDLOOP.

 MODULE LISTE_AFTER_LOOP.

 

2. The TOP Include

Developer Note: Centralizing the memory map and state-tracking variables ensures the PAI (Input) and PBO (Output) modules can share data seamlessly.

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& GLOBAL TYPES & VARIABLES FOR CUSTOM BUTTONS
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
" [CRITICAL] Define the exact structure of the TMG table.
" Standard SAP appends 'VIMFLAGTAB' (Action and Mark flags) to every row 
" in the background. If we don't include it here, our memory mapping will fail!
TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_data.
         INCLUDE STRUCTURE zsd_so_appr_mx_t. 
         INCLUDE STRUCTURE vimflagtab.
TYPES: END OF ty_data.

" Global trackers to remember the user's choices.
" This is required because TMG destroys standard filters when swapping 
" between Display and Change modes.
DATA: gv_custom_sort_field TYPE fieldname,
      gv_custom_sort_dir   TYPE char1,         " 'A' = Asc, 'D' = Desc
      gv_custom_filt_field TYPE fieldname,
      gr_custom_filt_range TYPE REF TO data,   " Dynamic generic reference for RANGE table
      gv_last_tmg_mode     TYPE char1.         " Tracks 'S' (Display) or 'U' (Change)

" Shared internal tables and work areas for processing the data
DATA: lt_extract TYPE TABLE OF ty_data,
      ls_extract TYPE ty_data,
      ls_total   TYPE ty_data.

" Field symbols for safe memory casting and dynamic evaluation
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ft_range>        TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
               <fs_value>        TYPE any,
               <fs_cast>         TYPE ty_data,
               <fs_extract_flat> TYPE c,
               <fs_total_flat>   TYPE c.

 

3. The PAI Module (Input Clicks)

Developer Note: This module handles the heavy lifting. It safely converts SAP's flat character strings into structured data to avoid Unicode dumps (UC_OBJECTS_NOT_CONVERTIBLE).

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Module  HANDLE_CUSTOM_CMDS  INPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE handle_custom_cmds INPUT.

  DATA: lv_field     TYPE string,
        lv_tab_name  TYPE string,
        lv_fieldname TYPE fieldname,
        dref_range   TYPE REF TO data,
        ls_tabfield  TYPE rstabfield. " STRICT TYPING: MUST be rstabfield for standard FM

  DATA: lo_type        TYPE REF TO cl_abap_typedescr,
        lo_elem_type   TYPE REF TO cl_abap_elemdescr,
        lo_struct_type TYPE REF TO cl_abap_structdescr,
        lo_table_type  TYPE REF TO cl_abap_tabledescr,
        lt_comp        TYPE cl_abap_structdescr=>component_table,
        ls_comp        TYPE cl_abap_structdescr=>component.

  " Find out exactly which column the user clicked on
  GET CURSOR FIELD lv_field.
  SPLIT lv_field AT '-' INTO lv_tab_name lv_fieldname.

  " ===================================================================
  " ASCENDING SORT BUTTON (ZASC)
  " ===================================================================
  IF sy-ucomm = 'ZASC'.
    IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lv_fieldname IS NOT INITIAL.
      
      " 1. Save state globally for the PBO module
      gv_custom_sort_field = lv_fieldname.
      gv_custom_sort_dir   = 'A'.

      CLEAR lt_extract.
      LOOP AT extract.
        " 2. [CRITICAL] Safely cast the flat 'extract' string to a character type
        ASSIGN extract TO <fs_extract_flat> CASTING TYPE c.
        
        " 3. Read the flat memory into our structured work area safely
        cl_abap_container_utilities=>read_container_c(
          EXPORTING im_container = <fs_extract_flat>
          IMPORTING ex_value     = ls_extract ).
        APPEND ls_extract TO lt_extract.
      ENDLOOP.

      " 4. Perform dynamic sort based on clicked column
      SORT lt_extract BY (lv_fieldname) ASCENDING.

      REFRESH extract.
      LOOP AT lt_extract INTO ls_extract.
        ASSIGN extract TO <fs_extract_flat> CASTING TYPE c.
        " 5. Write the structured data back into the flat background string
        cl_abap_container_utilities=>fill_container_c(
          EXPORTING im_value     = ls_extract
          IMPORTING ex_container = <fs_extract_flat> ).
        APPEND extract.
      ENDLOOP.

      " Reset scrollbar to the top
      tctrl_zsd_so_appr_mx_t-top_line = 1.
      MESSAGE 'Table sorted ascending!' TYPE 'S'.
    ELSE.
      MESSAGE 'Please place the cursor on a column to sort.' TYPE 'W'.
    ENDIF.
    CLEAR sy-ucomm. " Prevent standard SAP from throwing 'Invalid Function'
  ENDIF.

  " ===================================================================
  " DESCENDING SORT BUTTON (ZDSC)
  " ===================================================================
  IF sy-ucomm = 'ZDSC'.
    IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lv_fieldname IS NOT INITIAL.
      gv_custom_sort_field = lv_fieldname.
      gv_custom_sort_dir   = 'D'.

      CLEAR lt_extract.
      LOOP AT extract.
        ASSIGN extract TO <fs_extract_flat> CASTING TYPE c.
        cl_abap_container_utilities=>read_container_c(
          EXPORTING im_container = <fs_extract_flat>
          IMPORTING ex_value     = ls_extract ).
        APPEND ls_extract TO lt_extract.
      ENDLOOP.

      SORT lt_extract BY (lv_fieldname) DESCENDING.

      REFRESH extract.
      LOOP AT lt_extract INTO ls_extract.
        ASSIGN extract TO <fs_extract_flat> CASTING TYPE c.
        cl_abap_container_utilities=>fill_container_c(
          EXPORTING im_value     = ls_extract
          IMPORTING ex_container = <fs_extract_flat> ).
        APPEND extract.
      ENDLOOP.

      tctrl_zsd_so_appr_mx_t-top_line = 1.
      MESSAGE 'Table sorted descending!' TYPE 'S'.
    ELSE.
      MESSAGE 'Please place the cursor on a column to sort.' TYPE 'W'.
    ENDIF.
    CLEAR sy-ucomm.
  ENDIF.

  " ===================================================================
  " MULTIPLE SELECTION FILTER BUTTON (ZFIL)
  " ===================================================================
  IF sy-ucomm = 'ZFIL'.
    IF sy-subrc = 0 AND lv_fieldname IS NOT INITIAL.

      " 1. [CRITICAL] Use RTTS to dynamically find the Data Type of the clicked column
      lo_type = cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_name( lv_field ).
      lo_elem_type ?= lo_type.

      " 2. Construct the standard 4 fields of a RANGE table (SIGN, OPTION, LOW, HIGH)
      CLEAR lt_comp.
      ls_comp-name = 'SIGN'.
      ls_comp-type = cl_abap_elemdescr=>get_c( 1 ).
      APPEND ls_comp TO lt_comp.
      ls_comp-name = 'OPTION'.
      ls_comp-type = cl_abap_elemdescr=>get_c( 2 ).
      APPEND ls_comp TO lt_comp.
      ls_comp-name = 'LOW'.
      ls_comp-type = lo_elem_type. " Apply dynamic type here
      APPEND ls_comp TO lt_comp.
      ls_comp-name = 'HIGH'.
      ls_comp-type = lo_elem_type. " Apply dynamic type here
      APPEND ls_comp TO lt_comp.

      " 3. Generate the table structure in memory
      lo_struct_type = cl_abap_structdescr=>create( lt_comp ).
      lo_table_type  = cl_abap_tabledescr=>create( p_line_type = lo_struct_type ).

      CREATE DATA dref_range TYPE HANDLE lo_table_type.
      ASSIGN dref_range->* TO <ft_range>.

      " Save filter state globally
      gv_custom_filt_field = lv_fieldname.
      gr_custom_filt_range = dref_range.

      ls_tabfield-tablename = lv_tab_name.
      ls_tabfield-fieldname = lv_fieldname.

      " 4. Call standard SAP multiple selection popup
      CALL FUNCTION 'COMPLEX_SELECTIONS_DIALOG'
        EXPORTING
          title         = 'Filter Table'
          tab_and_field = ls_tabfield
        TABLES
          range         = <ft_range>
        EXCEPTIONS
          cancelled     = 1
          OTHERS        = 2.

      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        REFRESH extract.

        " 5. Evaluate ALL records in the TOTAL table against the user's filter
        LOOP AT total.
          ASSIGN total TO <fs_total_flat> CASTING TYPE c.
          cl_abap_container_utilities=>read_container_c(
            EXPORTING im_container = <fs_total_flat>
            IMPORTING ex_value     = ls_total ).

          ASSIGN COMPONENT lv_fieldname OF STRUCTURE ls_total TO <fs_value>.
          IF <fs_value> IS ASSIGNED.
            " If range is empty, show all. Otherwise, check if value falls in range.
            IF <ft_range> IS INITIAL OR <fs_value> IN <ft_range>.
              extract = total.
              APPEND extract.
            ENDIF.
          ENDIF.
        ENDLOOP.

        " 6. [CRITICAL] Synchronize TMG's global line counter so the scrollbar works properly
        DESCRIBE TABLE extract LINES maxlines.
        tctrl_zsd_so_appr_mx_t-lines = maxlines.
        tctrl_zsd_so_appr_mx_t-top_line = 1.

        IF maxlines = 0.
          MESSAGE w004(sv). " Standard msg: 'No entries found...'
        ELSE.
          MESSAGE 'Table filtered successfully!' TYPE 'S'.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
    ELSE.
      MESSAGE 'Please place the cursor on a column to filter.' TYPE 'W'.
    ENDIF.
    CLEAR sy-ucomm.
  ENDIF.

  " ===================================================================
  "  CLEAR FILTER BUTTON (ZCLR)
  " ===================================================================
  IF sy-ucomm = 'ZCLR'.
    " Clear all tracking variables
    CLEAR: gv_custom_sort_field, gv_custom_sort_dir, gv_custom_filt_field, gr_custom_filt_range.

    " Restore EXTRACT from TOTAL
    REFRESH extract.
    LOOP AT total.
      extract = total.
      APPEND extract.
    ENDLOOP.

    DESCRIBE TABLE extract LINES maxlines.
    tctrl_zsd_so_appr_mx_t-lines = maxlines.
    tctrl_zsd_so_appr_mx_t-top_line = 1.
    MESSAGE 'Filter cleared!' TYPE 'S'.
    CLEAR sy-ucomm.
  ENDIF.

ENDMODULE.

 

4. The PBO Module (Output Re-apply)

Developer Note: When a user clicks Display <-> Change, SAP executes the standard LISTE_INITIALISIEREN routine, which brutally wipes the EXTRACT table and rebuilding it from scratch, destroying our custom filters. This module detects the toggle and instantly restores everything!

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Module REAPPLY_CUSTOM_STATE OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE reapply_custom_state OUTPUT.

  " 1. Record the mode (Display/Change) when screen first loads
  IF gv_last_tmg_mode IS INITIAL.
    gv_last_tmg_mode = status-action.
  ENDIF.

  " 2. Detect if the user toggled the Display <-> Change button
  IF status-action <> gv_last_tmg_mode.
    gv_last_tmg_mode = status-action. " Update tracker to the new mode

    " =======================================================
    " RE-APPLY THE FILTER
    " =======================================================
    " If a global filter exists, execute the exact same logic from the PAI module
    IF gv_custom_filt_field IS NOT INITIAL AND gr_custom_filt_range IS BOUND.
      ASSIGN gr_custom_filt_range->* TO <ft_range>.
      REFRESH extract.

      LOOP AT total.
        ASSIGN total TO <fs_total_flat> CASTING TYPE c.
        cl_abap_container_utilities=>read_container_c(
          EXPORTING im_container = <fs_total_flat>
          IMPORTING ex_value     = ls_total ).

        ASSIGN COMPONENT gv_custom_filt_field OF STRUCTURE ls_total TO <fs_value>.
        IF <fs_value> IS ASSIGNED.
          IF <ft_range> IS INITIAL OR <fs_value> IN <ft_range>.
            extract = total.
            APPEND extract.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

      " Re-sync scrollbar logic
      DESCRIBE TABLE extract LINES maxlines.
      tctrl_zsd_so_appr_mx_t-lines = maxlines.
    ENDIF.

    " =======================================================
    " RE-APPLY THE SORTING
    " =======================================================
    " Re-execute the sort on whatever records survived the filter
    IF gv_custom_sort_field IS NOT INITIAL AND gv_custom_sort_dir IS NOT INITIAL.
      CLEAR lt_extract.
      LOOP AT extract.
        ASSIGN extract TO <fs_extract_flat> CASTING TYPE c.
        cl_abap_container_utilities=>read_container_c(
          EXPORTING im_container = <fs_extract_flat>
          IMPORTING ex_value     = ls_extract ).
        APPEND ls_extract TO lt_extract.
      ENDLOOP.

      IF gv_custom_sort_dir = 'A'.
        SORT lt_extract BY (gv_custom_sort_field) ASCENDING.
      ELSE.
        SORT lt_extract BY (gv_custom_sort_field) DESCENDING.
      ENDIF.

      REFRESH extract.
      LOOP AT lt_extract INTO ls_extract.
        ASSIGN extract TO <fs_extract_flat> CASTING TYPE c.
        cl_abap_container_utilities=>fill_container_c(
          EXPORTING im_value     = ls_extract
          IMPORTING ex_container = <fs_extract_flat> ).
        APPEND extract.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDIF.

    tctrl_zsd_so_appr_mx_t-top_line = 1.
  ENDIF.

ENDMODULE.

 

Conclusion

Adding custom logic to SM30 requires deep knowledge of how SAP handles memory casting and standard GUI statuses. By leveraging RTTS, field symbol casting, and PBO state tracking, you can deliver an incredibly robust user experience that never dumps.

I hope this "every 1%" guide saves you hours of debugging! What are your favorite SM30 workarounds? Let me know in the comments below. 👇

— Ravindu Chamod Wickramage —

entry.title: Using Claude Code and Natural Language to Create SAP Datasphere Artifacts
entry.content:
How It Started

A few weeks ago, I tried an AI tool called Trae_CN to create Datasphere artifacts automatically. After a few hours of testing, it worked well! I was excited and wrote a blog post about it.

AI-Assisted SAP Datasphere Modeling

Then my teams chat filled up with messages from the security team.

The problem? Trae_CN wasn't approved by the company.

So I switched to Claude Code and tried again. This time, I wanted to solve a harder problem: creating complex models automatically. I focused on Analytic Models - the kind that everyone needs but finds hard to automate. These models need many steps: linking tables, setting up measures, and configuring business layers. Usually, you have to do all this by hand in the UI.

If we can use natural language to create these complex models, that would really save time.

I. The Problem: Too Much Manual Work

Data engineers spend a lot of time creating artifacts in SAP Datasphere.

When projects get big:

  • One data warehouse: 50+ tables, 30+ views
  • Complex analytic models: 10+ linked tables

Each artifact needs manual work: open UI → fill in fields → set up links → save

One table takes 5 minutes. 50 tables = 4 hours.

And many of these objects follow the same patterns. They can be automated.

II. The Idea: Use Natural Language

The ideal way should be like this:

"Create a customer table with ID, name, email, and city"
"Create a view from the customer table, show only ID and name"
"Create an analytic model linking customer and product tables"

Just say what you need, and it's done automatically.

III. The Solution: Claude Code + Skills + Datasphere CLI

How It Works

Claude Code understands your words and calls Skills that connect to Datasphere CLI to create artifacts.

The steps:

  1. You describe what you need in plain language
  2. Claude Code understands what you mean
  3. It calls the right Skill
  4. The Skill connects to Datasphere using SAP's official @sap/datasphere-cli tool
  5. It makes the JSON and creates the artifact

An Example

You say:

"Create a customer table with ID, name, and email. ID is the key."

Claude Code runs:

/create-local-table --name CUSTOMER --columns ID:String:10:key,NAME:String:100,EMAIL:String:100

The Skill creates this JSON:

{
  "definitions": {
    "CUSTOMER": {
      "kind": "entity",
      "elements": {
        "ID": { "type": "cds.String", "length": 10, "key": true },
        "NAME": { "type": "cds.String", "length": 100 },
        "EMAIL": { "type": "cds.String", "length": 100 }
      }
    }
  }
}

Done.

Why Natural Language Works

You can say it different ways:

  • "Make a table with order number, amount, and date"
  • "I need a table for customer information"
  • "Create a sales data table"

Claude Code understands all of these and calls the right Skill.

The Big Challenge: No Format Guide

Problem: Datasphere CLI docs don't show you how to write the CSN format for Replication Flow.

What to do?

Let AI learn it.

The method is simple:

  1. Create an example artifact in Datasphere UI by hand
  2. Use CLI to read that object's full structure
  3. Let Claude Code look at the CSN and find the patterns
  4. Use that template to make new artifacts
# Read an existing artifact
datasphere objects views read --name EXISTING_VIEW

# Claude Code helps figure out:
# - What fields are required?
# - What can we skip?
# - What are the rules?

This "reverse engineering" method helped me learn the right format for all artifact types.

IV. What I Built: 6 Skills, Focus on Analytic Models

The Skills I made:

Skill What It Does

create-local-tableMakes tables
create-viewMakes views
create-analytic-modelMakes analytic models
create-data-flowMakes data flows
create-replication-flowMakes replication flows
create-transformation-flowMakes transformation flows

Analytic Models

Why focus on this one? Because it's the most complex.

Analytic models need:

  • Linking fact and dimension tables
  • Setting up measure calculations
  • Choosing dimension fields
  • Setting up business layers

The old way to create an analytic model:

  1. Drag dimension tables
  2. Set up link fields
  3. Pick dimension fields
  4. Define measures
  5. Save and deploy

Takes 10-20 minutes.

The new way:

/create-analytic-model --name AM_SALES \
  --source SALES_FACT \
  --measures AMOUNT:sum,QUANTITY:sum \
  --dimensions CUSTOMER_ID:DIM_CUSTOMER:ID:NAME,CITY;PRODUCT_ID:DIM_PRODUCT:ID:NAME,CATEGORY

Done in 30 seconds.

It does this automatically:

  • Links customer dimension (using CUSTOMER_ID)
  • Links product dimension (using PRODUCT_ID)
  • Adds the dimension fields you want (NAME, CITY, CATEGORY)
  • Sets up measure calculations (sum of AMOUNT and QUANTITY)

V. Real Results: Talk in Natural Language

Creating a Sales Analysis Example

You tell Claude Code:

"Create a sales analysis data model for me.

First, make a sales fact table with order number, customer ID, product ID, and amount.

Then make a customer dimension table with ID, name, and city.

Next, make a product dimension table with ID, name, and category.

Finally, make an analytic model based on the sales fact table, linking these two dimensions, with amount sum as the measure."

Claude Code does this:

# 1. Create sales fact table
/create-local-table --name SALES_FACT \
  --columns ORDER_ID:String:10:key,CUSTOMER_ID:String:10,PRODUCT_ID:String:10,AMOUNT:Decimal:15:2

# 2. Create customer dimension table
/create-local-table --name DIM_CUSTOMER \
  --columns ID:String:10:key,NAME:String:100,CITY:String:50

# 3. Create product dimension table
/create-local-table --name DIM_PRODUCT \
  --columns ID:String:10:key,NAME:String:100,CATEGORY:String:50

# 4. Create analytic model
/create-analytic-model --name AM_SALES \
  --source SALES_FACT \
  --measures AMOUNT:sum \
  --dimensions CUSTOMER_ID:DIM_CUSTOMER:ID:NAME,CITY;PRODUCT_ID:DIM_PRODUCT:ID:NAME,CATEGORY

What happens:

  • You describe what you need in plain language
  • Claude Code understands and breaks it into steps
  • It calls the right Skills
  • Your whole data model is built

Time to build:

  • Old way: 2-3 hours
  • Natural language + Claude Code: 1 minute

VI. Open Source Project

The code is on GitHub: https://github.com/yuyonggang/dsp-cli

What's included:

  • 6 complete Skills
  • Full instructions (in English and Chinese)
  • Setup guide
  • Security guide

You can use it as is or change it for your needs.

I built this whole project using Claude Code — AI helped from start to finish.

Final Thoughts

What to Know

1. This Is Still New

Right now, the Skills work best for simple models. For complex business cases:

  • You need to test and try things
  • You might need to fix problems by hand
  • The Skills need more work

This is a step-by-step process, not instant.

2. Be Careful with Big Projects

If your project is complex:

  • Start with simple cases to test
  • Move to harder models slowly
  • Leave time for fixing issues

What's Next

My goal:

Give it a design doc → It creates everything

For example, give an AI Agent tool (like Claude Code) a requirements document:

Sales Analysis System Design
- Sales fact table: order, customer, product, amount, date
- Customer dimension: ID, name, city, country
- Product dimension: ID, name, category, price
- Analytic model: multi-dimension sales analysis

The AI Agent would:

  1. Read the document
  2. Create all tables and dimensions
  3. Build analytic models
  4. Hit an error? Read the error message
  5. Check API docs to understand why
  6. Fix it and try again

Fully automatic, no human help needed.

Using AI Agents to understand needs, work on their own, and fix their own mistakes in Datasphere projects.

entry.title: Issue with OData Service /CPD/SC_PROJ_ENGMT_CREATE_UPD_SRV - Error 414: Resource Type limitation
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I am encountering a blocking issue while trying to create a Customer Project using the OData service /CPD/SC_PROJ_ENGMT_CREATE_UPD_SRV (ProjectSet entity).

Context
I am sending a deep insert payload to create a project, its work packages, and the associated resource demands. My payload includes three different resource types:

  • 0ACT (TEAMS)
  • 0ADL (Additional Resource)
  • 0EXP (Expenses)

The Problem
The API call fails with the following error message:

Error Code: /CPD/SS_MESSAGES/414 Message: "You can create or update resource demands only for resource type 0ACT."

Questions

  • Is this a standard limitation of the SC_PROJ_ENGMT_CREATE_UPD_SRV service where it only supports labor-based resources (0ACT)?
  • If so, what is the recommended approach or alternative API to include Expenses (0EXP) and Additional Resources (0ADL) during the project creation phase?

 

Any guidance or workaround would be greatly appreciated as this is a critical part of our integration.

Best regards,

entry.title: Failed work orders / Why have maintenance plans stopped generating work orders?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Masters,
I recently noticed that some maintenance plans had stopped generating work orders, even though they were being generated correctly until last year: why is this happening?

When I analysed this in detail, I even noticed that two maintenance plans
> created in exactly the same way, on the same day, with only a 30-day difference in the issuance of work orders.
... only maintenance plan ‘A’ was still generating its work orders, whilst ‘B’ had stopped

How is this possible??

Or, what should I check in the completed work orders?

Or in the maintenance plans?? Although, as I said: they are identical!

Thank you for your help in resolving this issue 🙂

entry.title: Re: Not able to install Desktop Agent - Integrity Issue
entry.content:
follow these instruction:Registry key to add before launching Agent installation process

Computer\HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\SAP\Intelligent RPA\Desktop Agent

 

Key : Chk_MT (String type)

Value : false

 

Please follow this process

  • Uninstall manually the Agent (if still there)
  • Add the key Chk_MT inside the registry
  • Launch the installation of your Agent as “per machine” (installation choice only available by launching the .exe with admin rights)

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP BW with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @swati_gawade 

your blog reflects the current state quite well. However, I think you missed 2 things:

1) Extended maintenance until end of 2030 is available also if you are not on private Cloud, see: https://me.sap.com/notes/1648480 

2) What you, and SAP as well is always missing, is the second most important topic after BW modernization: Front-End modernization or Front-End transition. It is right, you can lift & shift your BW, but one day you will have to change the data source of all your ~500 to 1000 BW queries to Analytic Models ... and exchange it in your ~100 SAC stories.

I think this is the biggest pain point and most relevant reason why many customers are still reluctant. Many customers are trying to postpone this as far as possible, due to the never-ending crisis we are facing today.

Maybe you want to include the front-end topic into your paths as well?

BR, Martin

entry.title: New POD 2.0 Framework: POD Notifications
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series on SAP Digital Manufacturing new POD 2.0 framework, the new generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing worker UIs.

New POD 2.0 Framework

  1. The New Generation of SAP Digital Manufacturing Worker UIs
  2. Template-Based Design Instead of POD Types
  3. Visual WYSIWYG Designer
  4. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 1
  5. POD 2.0 Hands-on, Flexible Layout Capabilities, Part 2
  6. Custom UI Dialogs and Calling Production Processes
  7. Dynamic PODs, How to Dynamically Show, Hide, Enable, and Disable UI Elements
  8. Other Business Capabilities Enabled by POD 2.0
  9. Interactive Value Journey (IVJ)
  10. POD Notifications

🗓️ Timeline

POD 2.0 is currently in BETA with selected customers, General Availability (GA) is planned for 2605 release. BETA content is subject to change!

POD Notifications

POD Notifications in SAP Digital Manufacturing (SAP DM) are real-time alerts and messages delivered directly to operators working on Production Operator Dashboards (PODs). They provide instant communication about production events, quality issues, equipment status changes, and other critical manufacturing updates that require operator attention or action.

Legacy Setup

In POD 1.0, POD Notifications needed to be manually and individually enabled in POD Designer.

POD 2.0 Setup

In POD 2.0, enabling individual POD Notifications is no longer required, and 2.0 PODs are always connected and 'listening'.

To setup POD Notifications in POD 2.0, first you need to create a production process and ideally define minimum parameters so notifications can be dynamic.

Select 'Start' and 'Input Parameters' to create some input parameters.

Then, select 'POD Connection', 'POD Version' as '2.0', and map the input parameters.

Next, add a 'POD Message' control, and configure it accordingly by enabling/disabling 'Require Confirmation' and entering a 'Message', which can use any available parameters.

That´s it! Save it, deploy it and run it by entering any input parameters created.

If 'Require Confirmation' is checked, you must get a notification that requires a confirmation like the one below.

If 'Require Confirmation' is unchecked, you must get a regular toast notification like the one below.

 

Have you tried POD 2.0 Notifications? Let me know!

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: What is a Fleet Management System (FMS)?
entry.content: An FMS monitors vehicle health, driver behavior, fuel usage, and real-time tracking. Taabi provides advanced fuel tracking and vehicle monitoring for fleets.

entry.title: Sap Commerce Hac extension not working after 2211 jdk 21 upgrade
entry.content:
Hi,

Last year our customer's project team built an SAP Commerce Administration Console (Hac) extension based on this guide https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/9b5366ff6eb34df5be29881ff55f97d2/df4994dfb4464ede8ffe61366c9cff5b.html?locale=en-US  to flush cache cluster-wide.

This extension enables a new tab:

[
  {
    "basePath": "/cluster",
    "mainTabLabel": "Cluster",
    "subTabs": [
      {
        "path": "/monitoring/",
        "label": "Cache Monitoring",
        "skipPrefix": false
      }
    ]
  }
]

After we upgraded our custom code to version 2211 jdk 21 I now receive Error 404 when accessing the tab:

https://localhost:9002/hac/cluster/monitoring/

DEBUG [hybrisHTTP9] [FilterChainProxy] Secured GET /cluster/monitoring/
DEBUG [hybrisHTTP9] [FilterChainProxy] Secured GET /cluster/monitoring/
DEBUG [hybrisHTTP9] [DispatcherServlet] GET "/hac/cluster/monitoring/", parameters={}
DEBUG [hybrisHTTP9] [DispatcherServlet] GET "/hac/cluster/monitoring/", parameters={}
DEBUG [hybrisHTTP9] [DispatcherServlet] Completed 404 NOT_FOUND
DEBUG [hybrisHTTP9] [DispatcherServlet] Completed 404 NOT_FOUND

 

Our controller is built like this:

package de.hybris.platform.hac.controller;


import de.hybris.platform.core.model.user.UserModel;
import de.hybris.platform.hac.facade.HacCacheFacade;
import de.hybris.platform.hac.facade.HacClusterFacade;
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.user.UserService;
import java.lang.invoke.MethodHandles;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.Collection;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.Map;
import jakarta.annotation.Resource;
import org.slf4j.Logger;
import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Autowired;
import org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value;
import org.springframework.stereotype.Controller;
import org.springframework.ui.Model;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ModelAttribute;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMethod;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestParam;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ResponseBody;


@Controller()
public class ClusterViewController extends ClusterHacController {

  private static final Logger LOG = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MethodHandles.lookup().lookupClass());

  private static final String VIEW_BASE = "monitoring/cluster/";
  private static final String nodeGroupAll = "all";
  private final HacClusterFacade clusterFacade;
  private final HacCacheFacade cacheFacade;
  @Value("${hac.extlinks.wiki.cluster}")
  private String wikiCluster;
 

  @Autowired
  public ClusterViewController(final HacClusterFacade clusterFacade,
      HacCacheFacade cacheFacade) {
    this.clusterFacade = clusterFacade;
    this.cacheFacade = cacheFacade;
  }

  @RequestMapping(value = "/cluster/monitoring")
  public String cluster(final Model model) {
    model.addAttribute("wikiCluster", wikiCluster);
    model.addAttribute("regions", cacheFacade.getCacheRegions());
    return "clusterview";
  }

[...]


}

We do not receive any warning and the extension is correctly added to localextensions.xml. Is there anything I can check on the spring configuration? 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: How to verify email delivery for invoices in S/4HANA...
entry.content:
The Monitoring Email Transmissions app shows "OK" when the message was successfully handed over to the configured mail server (SMTP).  It does not confirm that the recipient’s mailbox exists, nor that the email was actually delivered or opened.  If the recipient’s address is invalid, the mail server may still accept the message and only later generate a bounce notification. That bounce is not reflected back into the S/4HANA Cloud monitoring app.

In order to handle the bounced mails, as you might be knowing, the bounce message will typically be sent back to the sender’s email address (or a designated return-path address configured in your mail server). To monitor these, you need to check your mail server logs or configure bounce-handling in your email infrastructure (e.g., Microsoft Exchange, SAP Business Technology Platform mail service, or another SMTP provider). Few clients set up dedicated mailboxes to collect bounce notifications and then integrate that information back into SAP (via custom development or middleware).

entry.content:
@gerardo_martingozalo 

This means that the checked system (on the other end of the RFC destination) does not support object sets via OSL because its release is too low. The ATC supports OSL-based object sets only starting from SAP_BASIS release 7.57.

entry.title: Re: Asset transfer ABUMN
entry.content:
Can you give an example ?

As per the current process, after an asset transfer is performed, the old asset will continue to run depreciation for the current year. Depreciation will not be posted on the new asset during the same year.

Starting from the next fiscal year, depreciation will stop on the old asset and will begin on the new asset.

entry.title: Custom fields for Purchase Order - Not updating in Fiori
entry.content:
Hi All,

we are on S/4HANA 2023 SP01 on premise system. Currently we are implementing requested extension of Purchase Order Item by our biznis. 
Requested change is pretty straightforward: Add two custom fields to Purchase Order Item. One character like and second one date. When first field is filled automatically fill second field with current date. This should work for ME21N transaction as well as for Fiori F0842A Manage Purchase Orders.

I was able to sucessfully add two custom fields to the object via Custom Fields and Logic Fiori application to the ME21N and also to the F0842A Manage Purchase Orders item view (using Key User Extensibility).

I was also able to define logic to fill new custom date field when custom character field is filled. I have used BADI MM_PUR_S4_PO_MODIFY_ITEM for this which is working nicely for ME21N. 

Issue is that when custom character field is changed in Fiori application F0842A, BADI is called and content on custom field on backend gets changed but change is not propagated to the frontend. There is no entity refresh done. User therefore does not see that the second custom field is filled. If the user does some change on standard PO item field the full entity refresh is done and second custom filled is filled only then. This would cause lot of confusion and we dont want to force user to edit standard fields just to see changes in custom fields.

I tried to add annotation to the ODATA service  MM_PUR_PO_MAINT_V2_SRV that is ODATA service used by Fiori application to implement side effects to custom fields but I am not albe to make it work. 

I tried:

  • enhancing MPC_EXT Define method of service
  • adaptation project via VS Code adding local annotation file

 

Annotations Target="MM_PUR_PO_MAINT_V2_SRV.C_PurchaseOrderItemTPType">
    <Annotation Term="Common.v1.SideEffects" Qualifier="RekChanged">
        <Record>
            <PropertyValue Property="SourceProperties">
                <Collection>
                    <PropertyPath>XY_REK_ID_PDI</PropertyPath>
                </Collection>
            </PropertyValue>
            <PropertyValue Property="TargetProperties">
                <Collection>
                    <String>XY_REK_DATE_PDI</String>
                </Collection>
            </PropertyValue>
        </Record>
    </Annotation>
</Annotations>

Non of these helped. Second custom field is not refreshed when first one is. Is there some way how to force refresh of one custom field when other custom field is change when Custom fields are used for PO Item. 

Thank you.

entry.title: Automatic Removal of Dunning Level in Business Partner When No Open Items Exist
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

we are currently using a dunning procedure with 4 dunning levels in our system.

At the moment, the behavior is as follows:
When a customer reaches the highest dunning level (level 4), this level remains stored in the Business Partner master data. It is only reset (e.g., from level 4 back to level 1) when a new invoice is created and subsequently dunned again.

We would like to change this behavior.

If a customer has no open items left (i.e., all outstanding invoices have been fully paid), the dunning level should be automatically removed/reset in the Business Partner master data.

This should apply to all dunning levels, for example:

  • If a customer is on dunning level 4 and clears all open items → dunning level should be removed

  • If a customer is on dunning level 2 and clears all open items → dunning level should also be removed

Is there a standard functionality or configuration option in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that supports this requirement?

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,

Nathalie

entry.title: Re: Business Address Services (BAS)
entry.content: SAP links SOPR entries to ADR6 through the partner’s address number. RSCONN01 just sends what’s already queued, so the email trigger usually comes from your SD output setup, not the batch job.

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh + AMQP: The Retry Trap Nobody Warns You About
entry.content:
My question is how can a message be resent again after the max number of retry is reached?

Let's say that there is a master data issue in the receiver system and this causes an error in the message delivery.  The retry count is 3 and the delay is 10 minutes.

Let's assume that the master data issue fixed after the retry count is reached ( after 30minutes ). So the message should be once again reprocessed to finally be delivered to the receiver system.

In option A, the message can be placed in DLQ JMS queue, if the maximum number of retry is reached. From that DLQ JMS queue it can be moved to the original JMS queue to be reprocessed for the 4th time.

But how can this be achieved in Option B? Because as far as I know the messages cannot be moved manually from a DLQ to the original processing queue.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price is not getting updated
entry.content:
Valuation category X means the material is managed with split valuation. This allows different valuation types (e.g., by origin, procurement type, or batch) to carry different prices. When you create a PO and GRN, the system updates the valuation type level, not the generic material master record. The material master price field you’re checking (in the general valuation view) will not change, because the system expects you to look at the valuation type-specific data.

To check the updated price, navigate to the Material Valuation app or the valuation data in the material master. Select the valuation type (e.g., "Imported," "Domestic," etc.) to see the updated moving average price or standard price. The generic material record will remain unchanged, since it’s only a header for the split valuation setup.

entry.title: Re: Margin taxation in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: Margin taxation is not natively supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Mostly it will be handled through external tax engines (like Vertex or Avalara) or custom configuration in the tax determination framework. The “best way” depends on whether you need compliance with EU margin schemes (e.g., travel services, second-hand goods) or local indirect tax rules.

entry.title: Re: Message type SHPCON for outbound delivery change whic...
entry.content:
The standard segment you are using (E1EDL18 with qualifier CHG) only updates header-level data. Batch split information, however, is maintained at the item level and requires different segments.

In order to meet your requirement, send the batch split information in E1EDL24 (for the sub-item) and E1EDL44 (for batch details). The parent item (main delivery item) remains in E1EDL18. Make sure the POSNR (item number) and UEPOS (higher-level item number) fields are set correctly so SAP knows the batch split belongs to the main item.

entry.title: Re: Credit check should not happen again, once Sale Order...
entry.content:
Standard SAP logic uses the confirmed quantity to determine the exposure, because that is what expected to be delivered. Even if you override LV_AMOUNT in BADI_SD_CM → FSCM_CREDIT_CHECK_ORDER, the framework still applies its own logic based on confirmed quantities unless you adjust the deeper determination routines.

What you can try is instead of only changing LV_AMOUNT, you need to adjust the credit check control logic in the BAdI so that the system considers ORDER_QUANTITY (from schedule lines) rather than CONFIRMED_QUANTITY. Key fields: XVBAP-BMENG (order quantity) vs. XVBEP-CMENG (confirmed quantity). Ensure you pass the recalculated value back to the credit check engine, not just override LV_AMOUNT locally, but I would not recommend to consider this custom development.  Better try to convince the client to go with standard functionality.
